cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question. The question is asking what Robin learned from the story. The options are A to D. First, I need to recall the story. Robin's family was at a restaurant when two homeless boys came in. They were dirty and smelled bad, but they were kind and had blue eyes. They only wanted a cup of coffee to warm up. Robin's mother saw them and ordered more food to give to them. Robin watched and realized what unconditional love was. Now he saves his money and helps homeless people whenever he can. The key part here is when Robin "understood what the unconditional love was" after seeing his mother's actions. His mother helped the boys without expecting anything in return. So the lesson is about treating others kindly, especially those in need, regardless of their situation. Looking at the options: A) We should treat others kindly. This fits because his mother's action was an example of kindness. B) We shouldn't laugh at others. The story doesn't mention laughter, so this is probably not correct. C) We should save pocket money.
